topic: An artist discovers someone has tattooed their work
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elara traced the line of the charcoal sketch, the paper cool beneath her fingertips. A strange, almost prickly feeling began to crawl up her neck as she leaned closer to examine the detail in the corner of the piece. There, subtly woven into the background shading, was a pattern—a tattoo. It wasn't part of her own design; it was foreign, deliberate. Her breath hitched, a small, involuntary sound escaping her lips. She pulled back abruptly, her hand shaking so badly the charcoal snapped. The air in the studio suddenly felt thick, suffocating. Who would do this? The implication settled over her like a heavy shroud, making the vibrant colors of her current work seem suddenly brittle and exposed.

[story 2]

Marcus stared at the canvas, his jaw tight. He’d spent weeks perfecting this portrait, pouring an intense vulnerability into the brushstrokes. Then, his gaze snagged on a mark near the subject’s wrist—a stark, intricate design that looked impossibly permanent. A cold wave washed over him, stealing the warmth from his chest. He felt a sudden, intense urge to cover the painting, to hide the evidence of this intrusion. He ran a hand over his face, feeling a tightness around his eyes. "Impossible," he whispered to the empty room, his voice thin and strained. The whole space seemed to vibrate with an unwelcome tension.

[story 3]

From the moment Leo saw the inked pattern overlaying his abstract landscape, a strange hollowness opened up in his core. It wasn't anger; it was a profound, unsettling quietude. He stood motionless, his feet rooted to the floor, the paint-splattered floor seeming miles away. He slowly raised a hand, not to touch the work, but to press it against his own forearm, feeling the slight, raised texture of the mark he’d just discovered. A tremor ran through his fingers. He needed distance, desperately needing to step away from the confrontation the image had ignited. The world outside the studio seemed too loud, too sharp.
